data.title: DALLAS FED RESEARCH: OVERALL BORROWING FOR US ECONOMY 'REMARKABLY STABLE' IN RECENT YRS AS PRIVATE SAVINGS BALANCES GOVT TRANSFER PAYMENTS/SUBSIDIES
Canada Caught In a 'Vicious Circle' of Weak Productivity, Bank of Canada Official Says — Update
data.description:
By Paul Vieira

OTTAWA--Canada is caught in a "vicious circle" of weak productivity that won't be broken without a regulatory overhaul to make the economy more conducive to business investment, a senior Bank of Canada official said.

Deputy Governor Nicolas Vincent is the latest senior central bank policymaker to sound the alarm on the need to reverse Canada's decades-long productivity slump, which he said is holding back wage growth and making the economy less resilient to shocks, like the abrupt shift in U.S. trade policy. He also made the link between improved productivity and keeping a lid on inflation.

"If we want to make things more affordable, we need to raise our income," Vincent said on Wednesday, according to prepared remarks set for delivery in Quebec City. "And the way to grow our income is by increasing productivity."

Vincent estimates that over a quarter century, each Canadian is about $5,000 poorer relative to their Group of Seven peers due to Canada's dismal productivity record.

"We're stuck in a vicious circle. When productivity is weak, it's much harder to meet current challenges and seize opportunities for the future," he said. "The problem is that too few Canadian companies are set up to face strong international competition. This is in large part because businesses in Canada have been investing too little for too long."

Vincent follows in the footsteps of Gov. Tiff Macklem and Senior Deputy Gov. Carolyn Rogers, who this fall also spoke about the need to address the issue. Productivity is a gauge of a country's living standards, as it measures how effectively an economy deploys labor and capital to produce a good or service.

To be sure, Prime Minister Mark Carney has taken notice. The former central banker in Canada and England said the government's annual budget plan, released this month, offers a blueprint to increase the level of business investment in the country, which has stagnated for about a decade. Government officials have identified lackluster business investment as a significant economic headwind.

Vincent said visits by central bank staff with business leaders underscores how corporate leaders believe Canada's regulatory system make it less conducive to investment. "This is not a trivial observation," he said. "Businesses often tell us that Canada's regulatory framework is too cumbersome, complex and far-reaching. They cite it as an irritant that increasingly prevents them from being more dynamic."

The Carney government has tried to address regulatory concerns with the creation of the Major Projects Office, which is aimed at fast tracking infrastructure and resource initiatives through the regulatory process.

Vincent added upgrades to national infrastructure, especially cross-country trade corridors, may also help on the productivity front, as well as a revamp of the tax system that ensures firms aren't punished financially for expanding operations.

Write to Paul Vieira at paul.vieira@wsj.com

Fitch Affirms Sicoob DTVM's Investment Management Quality Rating at 'Strong'
data.description:
Fitch Ratings-São Paulo-19 November 2025:

Fitch Ratings has affirmed Sicoob Distribuidora de Titulos e Valores Mobiliarios Ltda.'s (Sicoob DTVM) Investment Management Quality Rating at 'Strong'. The Rating Outlook is Stable.

Sicoob DTVM's rating applies only to its asset management activities and does not cover the fiduciary administration and custodian areas, which rely on separate processes and policies.

KEY RATING DRIVERS

Sicoob DTVM's 'Strong' rating reflects Fitch's opinion that the asset manager has strong investment capabilities and operational characteristics relative to its investment strategies, which are mostly comprised of conservative fixed income strategies.

Sicoob DTVM's rating reflects its well-defined fixed income investment process, an experienced executive team, robust risk and compliance controls, and substantial investments in technology since 2023. Sicoob DTVM's rating also considers in its strong shareholder, Banco Cooperativo Sicoob S.A. (Banco Sicoob; National Long-Term Rating AAA(bra)/Stable), the strong group's franchise and financial stability, and significant assets under management (AUM).

The revision of the Risk Management score from 'Strong' to 'Excellent' reflects the continuous improvement of processes and procedures with the implementation and updating of various systems, such as the risk management system.

Sicoob DTVM has posted significant growth in AUM (23.5% in 2024 and 11% in the first half of 2025) to BRL159 billion in June 2025, well above the Brazilian asset management industry. This increase has occurred due to the growth in deposits of the credit cooperative system in Brazil (Sicoob) which, in turn, allocates most of these funds with the manager.

In 2024, Sicoob DTVM launched three equity funds, bringing its total to four, and a foreign exchange and a credit rights investment fund (FIDC) in 2025. It has plans to establish another FIDC, two funds of funds (FoFs) and a real estate fund. To this end, the manager has invested in systems and hired professionals. Fitch continues to monitor the growth of these new strategies (less than 1% of AUM in June 2025) along with proportional developments in the manager's processes and resources.

In Fitch's opinion, Sicoob DTVM's main challenges include strengthening the investment team and research capacity, mainly for new strategies, consolidating the implementation of systems, especially the front-office system, and integration with third-party service providers.

The 'Strong' rating is based on the following assessments:

--Investment Process: Strong;

--Investment Resources: Strong;

--Risk Management: excellent (from Strong);

--Company and Client Servicing: Strong;

--Investment Performance: Consistent.

Investment Process

Fitch believes that Sicoob DTVM's investment process is well-structured, with well-defined strategies and limits, and based on regular committees that include the relevant professionals. The investment process is based on a top-down approach, primarily focusing on fixed income strategies, mostly focused on government securities and large financial institutions.

Sicoob DTVM has been improving its processes and systems since 2023 with pre-trading compliance, best execution and performance attribution, which favored decision-making and monitoring of positions. Expanding the analysis and research team would be positive, mainly for the new strategies. The manager does not have its own research team and uses research from specialized companies for macro and equities issues, and Banco Sicoob's credit team to evaluate corporate debt securities (mainly from banks and cooperatives).

All Sicoob DTVM funds were adjusted to the rules of CVM Resolution 175, which changed the regulatory framework for investment funds in Brazil and came into full effect on June 30, 2025.

Investment Resources

Sicoob DTVM's structure is adequate for its number of strategies but may need to be expanded for some of the new strategies, such as FIDCs and FoFs. The structure had 18 professionals in June 2025 and 2024, up from 14 in the previous year and 12 in 2022. The manager has three senior executives (the CIO, the director of administration/COO and the CRO) with significant professional experience and many years in the asset manager or in the group, reporting to related areas and committees of the group.

The investment structure includes a CIO, an equity portfolio manager, a fixed income portfolio manager, four traders, and an assistant. It also includes six professionals for the fiduciary administration area, including an economist, most with extensive experience and several years with the company. The risk areas, compliance department, and internal audit are fully segregated (the two last are provided by the group). Turnover was minimum in the last 36 months ended in June 2025.

Sicoob DTVM has made significant investments in systems since 2023 with the implementation of Bloomberg's front-office system, best execution, performance attribution, and Databricks database. The asset manager is also in the process of updating its market risk (from Luna to Argus) and middle and back-office (from YMF to other) systems. Bloomberg's front-office system is robust, with pre-trading, order execution, portfolio monitoring and operational processes. Previously, the asset manager did not have an order management system and used Excel spreadsheets.

Risk Management

Sicoob DTVM is overseen by several external entities (administrators, custodians, group risk and compliance areas, group internal audit, and external auditors of the asset manager and funds) including the Brazilian regulators. With the implementation of the systems, the group's risk management and compliance structures include pre-trading compliance among other automated controls. The structures have daily reports, monthly committees and strong controls for market, liquidity, operational and credit risks. Fund limit breaches were few and promptly resolved.

Company and Client Servicing

Sicoob DTVM was the 10th largest asset manager in Brazil, with AUM of BRL159 billion in June 2025, concentrated in fixed income strategies. Founded in 2005, the asset manager is a wholly owned subsidiary of Banco Sicoob. The bank was founded in 1996 and is part of the System of Credit Cooperatives of Brazil (Sicoob), the second largest credit cooperative system in the country by assets. Sicoob was the eighth largest financial institution in Brazil with total assets of BRL399 billion and a profit of BRL5.8 billion in June 2025 (net income of BRL8.3 billion in December 2024). It had 9.0 million members, 328 single cooperatives and 5,219 service points. The central and single cooperatives are Banco Sicoob's shareholders. The asset manager is highly integrated with the bank and holds much of Sicoob's liquidity.

Sicoob DTVM maintains low management fees, since its main objective is to provide services to associates at a low cost. In June 2025, AUM by type of investor was comprised of cooperatives (88%), medium-sized companies (4%), group pension fund (3%), retail (3%) and others (2%).

Investment Performance

The risk-adjusted performance of Sicoob DTVM's funds has been consistent and aligned with its objectives and relevant peers over the last 36 months ended in July 2025.

RATING SENSITIVITIES Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Negative Rating Action/Downgrade

Sicoob DTVM's rating may be sensitive to significant adverse changes in any of its key rating drivers mentioned above, especially in the case of a weakening of its financial profile, high key staff turnover, deterioration of its processes and policies, or expansion into more sophisticated strategies without commensurate enhancements in its processes and resources. A material deviation from Fitch guidelines for any key driver could lead to a rating downgrade.

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Positive Rating Action/Upgrade

The strengthening of its investment process and research team, consolidating the implementation of systems, especially front-office and back-office systems, and integration with external service providers could benefit the Investment Process and Investment Resources scores. Such improvements, taken together, could have a positive impact on the rating.

Sicoob Distribuidora de Titulos e Valores Mobiliarios Ltda.; Investment Management Quality Rating; Affirmed; Strong; Rating Outlook Stable

data.title: North Sea Crude-WTI Midland rises on a deal
data.description:
WTI Midland gains on a deal.

PLATTS WINDOW

* Indications are free on board (FOB) unless marked as cost, insurance and freight (CIF) or delivered at place (DAP).

* There were two deals on Wednesday.

* WTI Midland: Petroineos bought from Vitol for December 3-7 at dated Brent plus 70 cents CIF Rotterdam, or dated Brent minus 56 cents FOB, according to Reuters calculations. That's higher than a deal on Monday.

* WTI Midland: Mercuria bought from Vitol for December 3-7 at dated Brent plus 85 cents CIF Rotterdam, or dated Brent minus 41 cents FOB.

data.title: POWELL INDUSTRIES INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Powell Industries, Inc., a Delaware corporation specializing in custom-engineered equipment and systems for electrical energy distribution, control, and monitoring, has released its annual 10-K report. The report highlights the company's robust financial performance, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in a competitive industry. Powell primarily serves the oil and gas, petrochemical, electric utility, and commercial and industrial markets.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenues: $1,104.3 million, reflecting a 9% increase driven by strong project backlog and bookings.
  • Gross Profit: $324.4 million, representing a 19% increase due to higher revenues and improved gross profit margin.
  • Operating Income: $217.9 million, up from $178.8 million, primarily due to increased revenue and improved gross profit margin.
  • Net Income: $180.7 million, an increase from $149.8 million, driven by higher revenue and improved gross profit margin.
  • Diluted EPS: $14.86, compared to $12.29, reflecting the increase in net income.

Business Highlights

  • Business Overview: Powell Industries, Inc. develops, designs, manufactures, and services custom-engineered equipment and systems for electrical energy distribution, control, and monitoring. The company serves the oil and gas, petrochemical, electric utility, and commercial and industrial markets.
  • Recent Developments: In August 2025, Powell completed the acquisition of Remsdaq Limited, a UK-based manufacturer of SCADA RTUs, enhancing its automation platform capabilities.
  • Production Capacity Expansion: Powell announced a $12.4 million investment to expand production capacity at its Jacintoport manufacturing facility in Houston, Texas, adding 335,000 square feet of productive capacity and doubling the shoreline bulkhead length to support increased schedule flexibility.
  • Products and Services: Powell's principal products include integrated power control room substations, custom-engineered modules, electrical houses, distribution switchgear, medium-voltage circuit breakers, and monitoring and control systems. The company also provides field service inspection, installation, commissioning, modification, and repair services.
  • Revenue Segments: For the fiscal year ended September 30, 2025, revenue was distributed across several market sectors: oil and gas (37%), electric utility (25%), commercial and other industrial (16%), petrochemical (14%), light rail traction power (4%), and all others (4%).
  • Geographical Performance: International revenues increased by 35% in Fiscal 2025, driven by increased project volume from Canada operations and activity in the Middle East and Africa region.
  • New Product Launches: The acquisition of Remsdaq included technology valued at approximately $3 million, which is expected to enhance Powell's product offerings in SCADA RTUs.
  • Future Outlook: Powell's backlog increased to $1.4 billion as of September 30, 2025, with approximately $824 million expected to be recognized as revenue in Fiscal 2026. The company anticipates continued growth in the electric utility and commercial markets, with a focus on expanding into hydrogen production, carbon capture, and alternative fuels.
  • Market Trends: The oil and gas market is responding to increased international demand for LNG and gas-to-chemical processes. The electric utility market is experiencing a resurgence in power generation investment, and the commercial market is seeing growth in data centers and AI investments.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: The company completed the acquisition of Remsdaq Limited, a U.K.-based manufacturer, to expand its automation platform capabilities. This acquisition is part of a strategic initiative to enhance utility operational efficiency and system reliability. Additionally, the company announced a $12.4 million investment to expand production capacity at its Jacintoport manufacturing facility in Houston, Texas, which will support increased demand from oil and gas customers and other market sectors.
  • Capital Management: The company increased its revolving line of credit from $125 million to $150 million, with an extended expiry date to October 4, 2028, to enhance financial flexibility. It also maintained a strong cash position, with cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments increasing to $475.5 million. The company paid dividends totaling $12.9 million and managed its capital allocation to support organic and inorganic growth opportunities, including research and development initiatives.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to recognize approximately $824 million of its $1.4 billion backlog as revenue in the next fiscal year, indicating a strong pipeline of projects. It will continue to focus on strategic capital allocation to support growth in key markets, including electric utility and commercial sectors, while managing supply chain challenges and inflationary pressures. The company is also evaluating the impact of the One Big Beautiful Bill Act on its operations and financial results.

Challenges and Risks

  • Competitive Landscape: Powell Industries faces significant challenges in maintaining a competitive edge in a highly competitive industry. The company competes with larger multinational corporations like ABB, Eaton, Schneider, and Siemens Industries, Inc., which have greater global resources. The competitive landscape is influenced by factors such as technical support, engineering capabilities, and pricing.
  • Supply Chain Risks: The company's reliance on a limited number of suppliers for key components and raw materials poses a risk of supply chain disruptions, which could impact production costs and delivery schedules.
  • Market Risks: The cyclical nature of the end markets, such as oil and gas, petrochemical, and electric utility, can lead to fluctuations in demand for the company's products and services. Economic downturns, geopolitical conditions, and regulatory changes can further exacerbate these fluctuations.
  • Operational Risks: The company faces operational risks related to project execution, including the potential for cost overruns and delays. Supplier concentration and limited capacity may lead to disruptions in the supply chain, affecting the company's ability to meet customer commitments.
  • Technological Risks: The company is exposed to technological risks, as competitors may develop superior products or incorporate AI more effectively, potentially making Powell's offerings obsolete.
  • Commodity Price Volatility: The ongoing supply chain disruptions and high volatility in commodity prices, particularly for copper, aluminum, and steel, have increased production costs. The company is also navigating the impacts of U.S. global trade policy changes and potential international retaliatory measures, which could affect raw material costs and margins.
  • Market Risks: Powell Industries is subject to market risks, including fluctuations in foreign currency exchange rates, which could impact financial results. The company's international operations, particularly in the U.K. and Canada, expose it to risks related to political and economic instability, currency fluctuations, and compliance with foreign regulations.
  • Regulatory Risks: The company is monitoring the potential impacts of the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA) on its operations, which includes changes to tax provisions that could affect financial results and liquidity.

SEC Filing: POWELL INDUSTRIES INC [ POWL ] - 10-K - Nov. 19, 2025

data.title: Mueller Water Products, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Mueller Water Products, Inc., a leading provider of water infrastructure and flow control products, has released its annual Form 10-K report for the fiscal year 2025. The report highlights the company's robust financial performance, strategic initiatives aimed at enhancing operational efficiency, and the challenges it faces in a competitive market. This comprehensive summary provides an overview of the key financial and operational highlights, strategic initiatives, and risks outlined in the report.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales: $1,429.7 million, an increase of 8.7% from the prior year, primarily due to higher sales volumes and higher prices across most product lines.
  • Gross profit: $516.7 million, an increase of 12.6% from the prior year, driven by higher volumes, favorable pricing, and manufacturing performance efficiencies, partially offset by inflation and increased tariffs.
  • Operating income: $260.6 million, an increase from $181.7 million in the prior year, reflecting improved gross profit and reduced strategic reorganization and other charges.
  • Net income: $191.7 million, up from $115.9 million in the prior year, benefiting from higher operating income and lower interest expense.
  • Net income per basic share: $1.23, compared to $0.74 in the prior year, reflecting the increase in net income.
  • Net income per diluted share: $1.22, compared to $0.74 in the prior year, reflecting the increase in net income.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: Water Flow Solutions accounted for approximately 58% of fiscal 2025 consolidated net sales, while Water Management Solutions contributed about 42%.
  • Geographical Performance: Approximately 5% of Water Flow Solutions' net sales and 6% of Water Management Solutions' net sales were to Canadian customers in fiscal year 2025.
  • Sales Units: Water Flow Solutions recognized $824.9 million in net sales for 2025, with significant contributions from iron gate valves and specialty products. Water Management Solutions recognized $604.8 million in net sales, driven by hydrants and repair and installation products.
  • New Production Launches: The company transitioned production to a new state-of-the-art brass foundry in Decatur, Illinois, which is expected to improve operational efficiency and better serve service brass customers.
  • Future Outlook: For fiscal year 2026, the company anticipates consolidated net sales to increase between 1.4% and 2.8% compared to fiscal 2025, with continued demand in the municipal repair and replacement market and challenges from inflation and tariffs.
  • Operational Strategy: The company is focused on improving operational excellence, increasing supply chain efficiencies, and developing advanced manufacturing capabilities to drive productivity and increase domestic capacity.
  • Management Changes: In 2025, the company appointed Ms. Melissa Rasmussen as Senior Vice President and Chief Financial Officer and announced the retirement of CEO Ms. Marietta Edmunds Zakas, with Mr. Paul McAndrew set to succeed her.
  • Operational Challenges: The Israel-Hamas war in October 2023 caused a temporary shutdown of the facility in Ariel, Israel, impacting supply chain efficiency and leading to newly implemented tariffs on products imported into the United States.
  • Sustainability Initiatives: The company is committed to responsible sourcing and manufacturing sustainable products, with targets for reducing greenhouse gas emissions, water withdrawal intensity, and waste to landfill.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: The company has undertaken several strategic initiatives to enhance operational efficiency and expand its market presence. Key initiatives include the transition of production to a state-of-the-art foundry to improve operational efficiency and better serve service brass customers. The company is also investing in its foundries to expand capacity and increase manufacturing efficiencies, positioning itself strategically in response to geopolitical and tariff uncertainties. Additionally, the company has expanded its supplier base and improved throughput to mitigate operational risks following supply chain challenges due to geopolitical events.
  • Capital Management: The company has been actively managing its capital through a combination of debt and equity strategies. It maintains a revolving credit facility with a borrowing capacity of $175 million, maturing in 2029, and has issued $450 million of 4.0% Senior Unsecured Notes maturing in 2029. The company repurchased $15 million of its common stock during the fiscal year and has $65 million remaining under its share repurchase authorization. Dividends declared per share increased to $0.268, reflecting a commitment to returning value to shareholders. The company also maintains a strong liquidity position with $431.5 million in cash and cash equivalents and additional borrowing capacity under its asset-based lending arrangement.
  • Future Outlook: Looking ahead to fiscal year 2026, the company anticipates a consolidated net sales increase of 1.4% to 2.8% compared to fiscal 2025. The company expects to continue facing challenges related to government policy changes, supply chain disruptions, and inflationary pressures. It plans to address these challenges through pricing actions, cost containment measures, and supplier management. The company also expects to invest between $60 million and $65 million in capital expenditures to expand production capacity and enhance operational capabilities, particularly in its iron foundries. The company remains focused on maintaining a resilient demand in the municipal repair and replacement market, driven by aging infrastructure and increasing water rates.

Challenges and Risks

  • Market Risks: The company's financial performance is heavily dependent on local, state, and federal spending on water and wastewater infrastructure. Economic downturns or changes in government spending priorities could adversely affect sales.
  • Operational Risks: The company relies on a small group of key customers for a significant portion of its sales, and any loss of these customers could impact financial results. Additionally, the company faces risks related to supply chain disruptions, particularly from single-source or limited-source suppliers.
  • Regulatory Risks: Compliance with stringent environmental, health, and safety laws imposes significant costs. Changes in these regulations or failure to comply could result in penalties or operational disruptions.
  • Emerging Risks: The company is in the early stages of incorporating AI into its operations, which presents risks related to technological failures and competitive disadvantages if not effectively managed. Additionally, the legal and regulatory landscape for AI is rapidly evolving, which could impact the company's operations.
  • Geopolitical Risks: The Israel-Hamas conflict has caused temporary shutdowns in the company's facilities in Israel, highlighting the risk of geopolitical events disrupting operations.
  • Management's Focus: Management is focused on improving operational excellence and expanding capabilities through disciplined investments in manufacturing and product development. The company is also prioritizing customer experience and innovation to drive sales growth. However, management acknowledges the challenges posed by inflation, supply chain disruptions, and labor shortages, which could impact profitability.
  • Market Risk Disclosures: The company is exposed to foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, particularly as it expands its international operations. This exposure could significantly impact financial results, especially if the distribution of operations outside the United States increases. Additionally, the company faces risks related to changes in interest rates and credit markets, which could affect project costs and financial performance.

data.title: T.J. Maxx Owner Still Drawing Budget-Conscious Shoppers Ahead Of Holiday Season — Update
data.description:
By Connor Hart

T.J. Maxx parent TJX Cos. said it expects a strong holiday season as the off-price retailer continues to win over inflation-weary shoppers with its value and deals.

The company, which also owns Marshalls and Homegoods, on Wednesday once again lifted its full-year outlook after companywide comparable sales climbed 5% during the latest quarter. The increase, which beat both company guidance and Wall Street expectations, was driven by gains in store traffic and average spend per customer.

"Clearly, our value proposition continued to resonate with consumers," Chief Executive Ernie Herrman said on a call with analysts, adding that the company expects to continue to steal market share from competitors globally.

"As to the fourth quarter, we are off to a strong start," Herrman said.

TJX guided for same-store sales, or those from stores and digital channels operating for at least a year, to rise 2% to 3% during the current quarter, which includes the all-important holiday shopping season.

That outlook came in below the 3.1% that Wall Street forecast. However, JPMorgan analysts said they weren't concerned, noting that the past four quarters Herrman described as being "off to a strong start" all went on to post comparable sales at or above the high end of management's guidance.

William Blair analysts similarly cheered the company's latest report, saying it continues to outperform high expectations despite a volatile macroeconomic backdrop.

For the year, TJX now expects comparable sales to increase 4%, compared with a prior outlook of up 3%. The company additionally raised its earnings outlook to $4.63 to $4.66 a share, from $4.52 to $4.57 a share.

Wall Street is modeling same-store sales to climb 3.4% for the year, and for earnings of $4.61 a share.

The new outlook came as TJX logged higher profit and sales during the recent quarter. The company notched quarterly earnings of $1.28 a share for its three months ended Nov. 1, ahead of the $1.23 a share that analysts had forecast.

Sales jumped 7.5% to $15.12 billion and topped the $14.85 billion that Wall Street had projected.

Write to Connor Hart at connor.hart@wsj.com

data.title: NYMEX Overview: Petroleum Futures Back Off Steep Early Losses After EIA Data — OPIS
data.description:
Oil and refined product prices were lower heading into Wednesday afternoon but were backing away from earlier steep losses following the release of Energy Information Administration data showing a solid decline in U.S. crude inventories.

U.S. crude prices were down about 2.1% at 11:15 a.m. ET, with the December contract down $1.37 to $59.37/bbl in light trading ahead of expiration following Thursday's session. The more-active January contract was $1.47 in the red to $59.20/bbl, with both West Texas Intermediate contracts about 60cts above earlier lows.

January Brent crude was seeing losses of $1.40 to $63.49/bbl while February prices were down $1.42 to $62.99/bbl. Most activity in refined products was also focused on the next-month contracts.

The December ULSD contract, which had sunk as low as $2.5608/gal earlier in the day, was down 9.04cts to $2.6107/gal, while January prices were 8.6cts lower to $2.5387/gal.

RBOB futures were having a tougher time recovering, with December prices down 5.91cts to $1.9402/gal, a little over 1ct off its previous low of $1.9294/gal. January prices were 5.41cts in the red to $1.8679/gal.

Energy prices had started the day pressured by an inventory report released Tuesday evening by the American Petroleum Institute showing across-the-board builds in crude, gasoline and distillate inventories. Prices were also pressured by reports that the Trump administration had sent Pentagon officials to Ukraine in another effort to broker an end to fighting between that country and Russia.

EIA, however, on Wednesday morning reported U.S. crude inventories fell by 3.4 million bbl during the week ended Friday, leaving them about 5% below the seasonal five year average. EIA also reported a 2.3 million bbl build in

gasoline inventories and a 200,000 bbl increase in distillate supplies during the week. Despite the increases, gasoline remains 3% below seasonal averages while distillate supplies are 7% lower.

The declines came as EIA reported U.S. implied gasoline demand - as measured by products supplied - fell by 500,000 b/d to 8.528 million b/d. The decline was expected, as the first week of November normally sees a high demand reading.

Last week's reading was above both the same week last year and in 2023.

Distillate demand also fell, dropping by 136,000 b/d to 3.882 million b/d. This was also above the same week last year but down by more than 300,000 b/d from the same week in 2023.Gasoline and diesel prices were also moving lower in spot markets around the country, dragged down by the weakness in futures. Gulf Coast CBOB and RBOB, however, were seeing losses of more than 10cts/gal, as a cycle change overnight added additional headwinds to prices.

This content was created by Oil Price Information Service, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. OPIS is run independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

  • Reporting by Steve Cronin, scronin@opisnet.com; Editing by Michael Kelly, mkelly@opisnet.com

data.title: Jobs Data Will Be Stale at Fed's December Meeting — Market Talk
data.description: The Bureau of Labor Statistics says it plans to publish October payrolls data at the same time as its November jobs report, which has been rescheduled until Tuesday, Dec. 16. The agency is catching up on data that was missed during the shutdown. That means when the Fed meets on Dec. 9 and 10 for its last meeting of the year, officials won't have access to October or November jobs data. The most recent data available to the Fed will be from the September jobs report, now scheduled for release tomorrow. (matt.grossman@wsj.com; @mattgrossman)

Henkel Delights U.S. Soccer Fans With Immersive Experiences at 'Henkel House', Showcasing How Winning Starts at Home
data.description:
• ‘Henkel House' features brands across Henkel's well-known consumer brand portfolio, including Dial® soap, Persil® laundry detergent, Loctite® adhesives and göt2b® hair products

  • Alexi Lalas, U.S Soccer Legend, joined fans in Tampa and shared passionate insights on the growth of soccer and excitement for 2026

ROCKY HILL, CT / ACCESS Newswire / November 19, 2025 / As a proud Official Partner of U.S. Soccer, Henkel is committed to helping Fans win - on and off the field, whether it's on gameday, or through small daily victories of life that start at home.

As part of this commitment, Henkel launched ‘Henkel House', an immersive modular experience for Fans, representing different areas of a home, from the garage, to the kitchen, to the laundry room.

Debuting at the U.S. Men's National Team match v Uruguay on November 18, 2025, in Tampa, Florida, excited Fans enjoyed the immersive Henkel House experience while interacting with Henkel's portfolio of iconic products in a soccer-themed setting, elevating popular rituals at each experience.

Dial® Up Your Gameday Clean Experience: A Backyard setting demonstrating

why Dial is the #1 Antibacterial Hand Soap Brand*

* In the U.S., based on Circana MULO+ dollar sales for the 52 weeks ending 11/09/2025

  • Building on its passion for Soccer, Dial® Backyard gives Fans the opportunity to participate in an educational Hand Wash experience where they add UV lotion on their hands, then place them inside a blacklight box (experiment to simulate bacteria). Fans then wash their hands with Dial® soap, and place them back in the box to see how Dial's antibacterial soap eliminates 99.9% of bacteria.

  • Creating a fun photo moment from their Dial® experience, Fans also celebrate in style by sliding down into a bubble pit, celebrating the joy of bubbles (which are iridescent balls).

  • Celebrating its passion for the U.S. Teams and giving back, Dial® is making a donation for every ‘Clean Sheet' in a U.S. Senior National Team soccer match. To-date, Dial® has donated $100,000 to DonorsChoose, an education nonprofit that supports public school teachers and students nationwide.

Persil® Laundry Room shows Fans how Persil keeps their jerseys looking fresh and clean

  • Energized Fans enjoy a visually engaging display of soccer jerseys - a dirt-stained jersey washed with Persil® Original liquid detergent showcases how Persil® offers a deep clean and care for fabrics on and off the field. Fans are also able to play Spin Cycle, a spin-to-win game where Fans win exclusive Persil® U.S. Soccer co-branded gear.

Loctite® Built to Perform Garage provides Fans with hands-on experiences to customize their Gameday

  • A hands-on DIY station where Fans customize their own co-branded giveaways using Loctite products. By choosing and applying patches, guests create a personalized keepsake from their U.S. Soccer watching experience.

  • An attention-grabbing photo moment that turns the traditional soccer field upside down, demonstrating the expertise of Loctite®, as the ultimate provider of strong, simple and long lasting bonds for all gluing needs. Guests step onto the flipped pitch and pose for an integrated photo kiosk. When participants receive their photo, it flips, appearing as though they're hanging upside down.

göt2b® Styling Station inspires Fans to get creative with their hair

  • Encouraging Fans to play bold but style bolder, they are cheered on to try new hairstyles. Fans make themselves truly Gameday Ready by selecting trendsetting upgraded game-day looks from a local stylist with creative göt2b products.

"Henkel and our family of brands are proud to present a fan experience where every room and brand tells a new story of performance, care, and style," said Jenny Schiavone, Vice President Corporate Communications, Americas at Henkel."Our partnership with U.S. Soccer is focused on supporting the exciting growth of the game in North America, and inspiring and engaging our customers and consumers with our family of brands. We are proud to be part of the U.S. Soccer family, and we support every fan, every day, through our commitment of Winning Starts at Home."

Alexi Lalas, capped 96 times by U.S. Soccer and a well-respected media personality, joined the fun with fans and shared his insightful views on soccer

"Fans love this great game of ours, and it was fun being at Henkel House, experiencing the energy as Fans enjoyed the immersive rituals of how Henkel's products help Fans to win, on and off the field. If I was playing today, Henkel's Dial® soap, Persil® laundry detergent, Loctite® adhesives and göt2b® hair products would be part of my daily regime, getting me gameday ready, " said Alexi Lalas, U.S. Soccer legend.

"2026 will be a once-in-a-generation moment for soccer in the U.S. Our U.S. team needs to feel a home field advantage. From my experience, it's not a pressure, it's a power. We're all together on the same team - Fans, Henkel and the U.S. Soccer family, to back the players and do all we can to ensure Winning Starts at Home. We have a responsibility to make sure it is an experience that is memorable for an entire generation."

Henkel plans to delight U.S. Soccer Fans in 2026 by bringing the Henkel House to select U.S. Soccer matches and introducing it to shoppers in its key markets, during the most exciting, unprecedented moment for the sport in the United States over the next several years.

Henkel's partnership with U.S. Soccer is its first-ever portfolio partnership, where a range of its more than 30 popular brands in North America, promote the U.S. Men's and Women's National Teams, as well as the Youth and Extended National Teams.

About Henkel in North America

Henkel's portfolio of well-known brands in North America includes all®, Purex® and Persil® laundry detergents, Snuggle® fabric softeners, Dial® soaps, Schwarzkopf® hair care, as well as Loctite®, Technomelt® and Bonderite® adhesives. With sales close to 6.5 billion US dollars (6 billion euros) in 2024, North America accounts for 28 percent of the company's global sales. Henkel employs around 8,000 people across the U.S., Canada and Puerto Rico. For more information, please visit www.henkel-northamerica.com and on X @Henkel_NA.

About Henkel

About Henkel With its brands, innovations and technologies, Henkel holds leading market positions worldwide in the industrial and consumer businesses. The business unit Adhesive Technologies is the global leader in the market for adhesives, sealants and functional coatings. With Consumer Brands, the company holds leading positions especially in laundry & home care and hair in many markets and categories around the world. The company's three strongest brands are Loctite, Persil and Schwarzkopf. In fiscal 2024, Henkel reported sales of more than 21.6 billion euros and adjusted operating profit of around 3.1 billion euros. Henkel's preferred shares are listed in the German stock index DAX. Sustainability has a long tradition at Henkel, and the company has a clear sustainability strategy with specific targets. Henkel was founded in 1876 and today employs a diverse team of about 47,000 people worldwide - united by a strong corporate culture, shared values and a common purpose: "Pioneers at heart for the good of generations." More information at www.henkel.com.

Photo material is available at www.henkel-northamerica.com/press

data.title: DJIA Falls 45.71 Points (0.10%)
Canada Caught In a 'Vicious Circle' of Weak Productivity, Bank of Canada Official Says
data.description:
By Paul Vieira

OTTAWA--Canada is caught in a "vicious circle" of weak productivity that won't be broken without a regulatory overhaul to make the economy more conducive business investment, says a senior Bank of Canada official.

Deputy Governor Nicolas Vincent is the latest senior Bank of Canada policymaker to sound the alarm on the need to reverse Canada's decadeslong productivity slump, which he said is holding back wage growth and making the economy less resilient to shocks, like the abrupt shift in U.S. trade policy. He also made the link between improved productivity and keeping a lid on inflation.

"If we want to make things more affordable, we need to raise our income. And the way to grow our income is by increasing productivity." said Vincent, according to prepared remarks set for delivery in Quebec City.

Write to Paul Vieira at paul.vieira@wsj.com

JSC UzAuto Motors Announces Result of Tender Offer
data.description:
ASAKA, UZBEKISTAN / ACCESS Newswire / November 19, 2025 / On 10 November 2025, JSC "UzAuto Motors" (the "Offeror") launched an invitation to the holders of its outstanding U.S.$300,000,000 4.85 per cent. Notes due 2026 issued by the Offeror (ISIN: XS2330272944 (Regulation S) and US46653NAA00 (Rule 144A)) (the "Notes") issued by the Offeror (the "Invitation").

The Invitation was made on the terms and subject to the conditions contained in the tender offer memorandum dated 10 November 2025 (the "Tender Offer Memorandum"). Capitalised terms used in this announcement but not otherwise defined have the meanings given to them in the Tender Offer Memorandum.

The Invitation expired at 5.00 p.m. (New York City Time) on 18 November 2025 (the "Expiration Deadline"). As at the Expiration Deadline, the Offeror has received valid tenders of U.S.$133,543,000 in principal amount of Notes for purchase pursuant to the Invitation. No Notes were tendered under the Guaranteed Delivery Procedures.

On 19 November 2025, the Offeror completed the issue of U.S.$350,000,000 7.375 per cent. Notes due 2030 and accordingly the New Financing Condition has been satisfied.

Accordingly, the Offeror hereby announces that it will accept all U.S.$133,543,000 in principal amount of Notes validly tendered for purchase pursuant to the Invitation. The Offeror will make payment for the Notes purchased pursuant to the Invitation on the Invitation Settlement Date on 20 November 2025. In addition, Noteholders whose Notes are purchased pursuant to the Invitation will be paid interest accrued and unpaid on the relevant Notes from (and including) the interest payment date for the Notes immediately preceding the Invitation Settlement Date to (but excluding) the Invitation Settlement Date.

The Offeror intends to cancel any Notes purchased pursuant to the Invitation and the principal amount of the Notes that will remain outstanding following any such cancellation is expected to be U.S.$ 166,457,000.

This announcement is made by Shokhrukhon Dadakhodjaev, Head of Investor Relations Department, on behalf of JSC "UzAuto Motors" and constitutes a public disclosure of inside information under Regulation (EU) 596/2014as it forms part of United Kingdom domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018.

data.title: Flushing Bank to Be Honored with the Bridge Builder Award at the 2025 Chinatown Partnership and Chinatown BID Gala
data.description:
UNIONDALE, NY / ACCESS Newswire / November 19, 2025 / Chinatown Partnership and the Chinatown Business Improvement District (BID) announced today that Flushing Bank has been selected as a distinguished honoree at the organization's annual gala this evening.

Flushing Bank will receive the Bridge Builder Award, an honor that recognizes organizations demonstrating exceptional commitment to cross boundaries and build bridges, fostering connection, and investing in the communities they serve. The award celebrates institutions that go beyond traditional service to build meaningful bridges among diverse neighborhoods, cultures, and stakeholders.

The gala will take place on Wednesday, November 19, 2025, at 6:30 PM at House of Joy, located at 28 Pell Street, in the heart of Manhattan's Chinatown.

"We are thrilled to recognize Flushing Bank for its outstanding contributions and long-standing commitment to the communities across New York," said representatives from Chinatown Partnership and Chinatown BID. "Their work exemplifies what the Bridge Builder Award represents - investing in people, places, and progress."

John Buran, President and CEO of Flushing Bank, stated, "It is an honor to be recognized by Chinatown Partnership and Chinatown BID with the Bridge Builder award. We will continue our commitment to building rewarding relationships and expanding banking services to individuals, families and businesses in the communities we serve."

About Flushing Financial Corporation

Flushing Financial Corporation is the holding company for Flushing Bank®, an FDIC insured, New York State-chartered commercial bank that operates banking offices in Queens, Brooklyn, Manhattan, and on Long Island. The Bank has been building relationships with families, business owners, and communities since 1929. Today, it offers the products, services, and conveniences associated with large commercial banks, including a full complement of deposit, loan, equipment finance, and cash management services. Rewarding customers with personalized attention through bankers who can communicate in the languages prevalent within these multicultural markets are what makes the Bank uniquely different. As an Equal Housing Lender and leader in real estate lending, the Bank's experienced lending teams create mortgage solutions for real estate owners and property managers both within and outside the New York City metropolitan area. The Bank also fosters relationships with consumers nationwide through its online banking division with the iGObanking® and BankPurely® brands.

Additional information on Flushing Bank and Flushing Financial Corporation may be obtained by visiting the Company's website at FlushingBank.com.
